name: mcp-builder
description: Comprehensive guide for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers with support for tools, resources, prompts, and authentication. Use when: (1) Creating custom MCP servers, (2) Integrating external APIs with Claude, (3) Building tool servers for specialized domains, (4) Creating resource providers for documentation, (5) Implementing authentication and security
capabilities: Create MCP servers with tools, resources, and prompts
Build REST API wrappers for external services
Design effective JSON Schema tool definitions
author: AutumnsGrove
securitySummary: Documentation and example code repository with no malicious behavior. Contains educational MCP server examples demonstrating legitimate capabilities (filesystem, network, env_access) with appropriate security practices including environment variable handling, input validation, and path traversal protection. Static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks flagged as Ruby execution, MD5 used for non-cryptographic cache keys, and legitimate configuration patterns for MCP server development.
valueStatement: Claude needs standardized access to external tools and data sources. This skill provides complete guidance for building Model Context Protocol servers that expose custom tools, resources, and prompts to Claude safely and securely.
userTitle: Build MCP Servers for Claude Integration
limitations: Requires Python 3.10+ and MCP SDK installation
Network access depends on server implementation
File system access is server-specific and configurable

useCases.title: API Integration Servers
useCases.description: Wrap REST APIs as MCP tools so Claude can query external services like GitHub, weather, or databases.

useCases.title: Documentation Resources
useCases.description: Create resource providers that give Claude access to internal documentation, knowledge bases, and guides.

useCases.title: Custom Tool Servers
useCases.description: Build specialized tool servers for domain-specific operations like database queries, cloud resource management, or CI/CD.

promptTemplates.title: Simple Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a minimal MCP server with one tool called search_database that accepts a query parameter and returns results. Show the complete Python code using the MCP SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Tool Definition
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to define a tool with proper JSON Schema including required fields, default values, and enum constraints. Give a complete example.

promptTemplates.title: API Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an MCP server that wraps a REST API with authentication. Include environment variable handling, error handling, and response formatting.

promptTemplates.title: Production Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure an MCP server for production use including rate limiting, logging, health checks, Docker deployment, and graceful shutdown.

bestPractices: Use environment variables for all secrets and API keys, never hardcode credentials
Validate all inputs with JSON Schema and implement additional runtime validation
Add path traversal protection and directory restrictions for file system access
antiPatterns: Hardcoding API keys or credentials in source code
Skipping input validation on tool arguments
Using relative file paths without security checks

faq.question: What languages support MCP server development?
faq.answer: Python and TypeScript have official MCP SDKs. Other languages can implement the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of tools an MCP server can provide?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but practical servers typically expose 5-20 tools for usability. Group related functionality into focused servers.

faq.question: How do MCP servers communicate with Claude?
faq.answer: MCP servers use stdio transport (local) or HTTP with SSE (remote) to exchange JSON-RPC 2.0 messages with clients.

faq.question: Is data sent to MCP servers secure?
faq.answer: MCP runs locally on your machine. Servers only receive data you explicitly pass. Use environment variables for secrets and validate all inputs.

faq.question: Why is my MCP server not appearing in Claude?
faq.answer: Check that the server starts correctly, uses absolute paths in configuration, and returns proper tool definitions from list_tools handler.

faq.question: How does MCP compare to function calling?
faq.answer: MCP provides a standardized protocol for tool discovery, authentication, and resource access. It works alongside function calling with richer capabilities.
